channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jackson Connor Reflects on Role in Groundbreaking LGBTQ+ Drama 'Tip Toe'
item.description: Jackson Connor, 23, discusses his role as George Goss in the critically acclaimed LGBTQ+ drama 'Tip Toe', created by Russell T Davies. The series, streaming on Channel 4, explores themes of queer iden...

item.title: Marie Spartali Stillman: Pioneering Female Artist of the Pre-Raphaelite Movement
item.description: Marie Spartali Stillman (1844–1927) was a British painter of Greek descent and one of the most accomplished female artists associated with the second generation of the Pre-Raphaelite movement. Born in...

item.title: Israel's Electric Vehicle Market Slows Below Global Average
item.description: Israel's electric vehicle market has declined below the global average of 14%, with EVs accounting for 11% of new car sales in 2026. This marks a sharp reversal from previous years of rapid growth, wh...

item.title: Borno Gov approves N1.65bn for 300 CJTF orphans' education
item.description: Borno State Governor, Prof. Babagana Umara Zulum, has approved releasing N1.649 billion to settle outstanding scholarship obligations for 300 orphans of Counter-Insurgency Volunteers and other student...

item.title: Niagara Falls Mayor Cites Public Opposition and Private Sector Offer as Observation Wheel Proposal Faces Cancellation
item.description: The controversial plan by the Ford government to build a second giant observation wheel in Queen Victoria Park, Niagara Falls, has faced significant opposition. Mayor Jim Diodati of Niagara Parks Comm...

item.title: Tanzania's Opposition Leader Urges Sabasaba Trade Fair to Align with National Vision 2050
item.description: Dk. Nchimbi, a prominent political figure in Tanzania, has called for transforming the annual Sabasaba trade fair from a traditional exhibition into an economic driver aligned with Tanzania’s Developm...

item.title: Australia and India finalise uranium export agreement
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi are set to finalise a landmark uranium export agreement, ending over a decade of delays since their nations signed a nuclear coo...

item.title: Lavender Nyarjera Addresses Relationship with Koffi Macadory
item.description: An audio clip has surfaced where social media influencer Lavender Nyarjera clarifies the reasons behind her decision to end her relationship with Luo musician Koffi Macadory. The conversation, shared ...

item.title: Nigel Farage Resigns from UK Parliament Over Financial Scandal
item.description: Nigel Farage, a key ally of US President Donald Trump, has resigned from the UK Parliament after facing a financial scandal investigation. The Reform UK leader stepped down to contest a by-election in...

item.title: US Charges Indian Police Officer for Extortion Allegations
item.description: The United States has charged Gurinderjit Singh, an Indian police officer, with allegedly extorting $400,000 from a Los Angeles family by threatening to file false murder cases against their relatives...

item.title: Standard Bank and Jozi My Jozi drive urban renewal through community-focused waste management
item.description: Standard Bank and Jozi My Jozi have launched a collaborative initiative to roll out locally designed waste bins across Johannesburg, aiming to foster civic pride and create safer, cleaner public space...

item.title: Senator Lacson Comments on Impeachment Trial Voting Thresholds
item.description: Senator Panfilo ‘Ping’ Lacson commended his fellow senator-judges for maintaining composure during the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, despite alleged lectures from a colleague. In a...

item.title: Ziarat SP Suspended Following Terrorist Attack on Police Post, Inquiry Committee Established
item.description: A terrorist attack on the Mangi Dam police post in Ziarat resulted in the martyrdom of nine police officials, including two station house officers, and the elimination of 15 alleged terrorists during ...

item.title: Grandmother Denied Medical Aid for Minor Cut, Relies on Neighbor for Help
item.description: An 84-year-old grandmother, Pauline Shillito, was refused assistance by both her local pharmacy and GP after cutting her finger while preparing a melon. Despite the injury being minor, the wound bled ...

item.title: PM Modi visits Indonesia's Prambanan Temple as India and Indonesia deepen cultural heritage cooperation
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the historic Prambanan Temple complex in Indonesia along with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto, offering prayers at the nearly 1,000-year-old Hindu temple ded...

item.title: Community Strong's Centrism Faces Challenges in Political Upheaval
item.description: The article discusses the formation of the Community Strong party by Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall, which positions itself as a centrist alternative in Australia's politically turbulent landscape....

item.title: Charli XCX Announces 'Music, Fashion, Film' Album with David Cronenberg Collaboration
item.description: Charli XCX has unveiled the tracklist for her upcoming album 'Music, Fashion, Film', which features a collaboration with renowned filmmaker David Cronenberg. The 11-track project includes the Cronenbe...

item.title: Kakamega Police Recover Stolen Cattle After High-Speed Chase
item.description: Police in Kakamega County recovered three suspected stolen cattle after a high-speed chase that ended with a vehicle crashing into a ditch on the Mumias-Ekero road. The incident began when a driver at...

item.title: Maine Democrats Seek Unified Candidate to Challenge Collins
item.description: The article discusses the political landscape in Maine as Democrats consider potential replacements for Senator Graham Platner, who faces pressure to withdraw from his Senate race. Dr. Nirav Shah, a c...

item.title: Dr. Jacob Cloete's Book Captures the Voices of South African Commuters in Bellville Taxi Journeys
item.description: Dr. Jacob Cloete, a political scientist from the University of the Western Cape, has authored 'Stories in a Bellville Taxi', a book that captures the everyday experiences of South African commuters. T...
